heading: Accounts of clerks of district courts
label: 8877 Accounts of clerks of district courts
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)7677.34 U.S.C. 1152R.S. 4644.and this section reflect modifications of R.S. 4644 effected by—34 U.S.C. 1152(1) , , which provided for fixed annual salaries for United States attorneys and marshals;Act of May 28, 1896, ch. 252, § 629 Stat. 179(2) , , which repealed all laws authorizing distribution of prize proceeds to captors;Act of March 3, 1899, ch. 413, § 1330 Stat. 1007(3) , , which provided that clerks of United States district courts should receive fixed annual salaries; andAct of February 26, 1919, ch. 49, § 140 Stat. 1182(4) , , which abolished the offices of assistant treasurers and distributed their functions.Act of May 29, 1920, ch. 21441 Stat. 654
Amendments2018— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 115–232section 7677 of this title

children.text: the amount deposited with the Treasurer or public depositary, subject to the order of the court in the cause; and
the amounts ordered to be paid therefrom as costs and charges.

children.label: b
children.text: The clerk shall draw the orders of the court for the payment of costs and allowances and for the disposition of the residue of the prize fund in each cause.
copies of final decrees in prize causes; and
a semi-annual statement of the amounts allowed by the court, and ordered to be paid, within the preceding six months to the prize commissioners and special prize commissioners for their services.
